content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
From 
File 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSIPN 
Kathryn E. Donovan (~ 
Date 
: 
october 25, 1985 
No. M-85-219 
Subject: 
Telephone Advice to John Hutton, city Attorney of Kings 
county 
On October 15, 1985, Mr. Hutton requested telephone advice 
regarding the duty of the city of Kings city to file campaign 
statements. 
The city has enacted an ordinance creating a 
redevelopment agency. 
This ordinance will be the subject of an 
upcoming referendum election. 
The city has requested the 
private law firm which would serve as legal counsel for the 
redevelopment agency to prepare arguments against the 
referendum for publication in the ballot pamphlet. 
Mr. Hutton 
asked whether the city could become an independent expenditure 
committee which must file campaign statements if the City 
spends $500 or more for the preparation of these ballot 
pamphlet arguments. 
I informed Mr. Hutton that we would not consider the city 
to be an independent expenditure committee if it spends $500 or 
more for the preparation of these ballot pamphlet arguments. 
I informed Mr. Hutton that we would not consider the city 
to be an independent expenditure committee if it spends $500 or 
more for the preparation of ballot pamphlet arguments against 
the referendum. 
Elections Code Sections 4015 and 4057 
specifically authorize the city council to submit arguments 
against a referendum for publication in the ballot pamphlet. 
Because this expenditure is specifically authorized by statute 
it is a legitimate expenditure for the performance of the 
official duties of the city council, and should not be 
considered an independent expenditure under Government Code 
section 82031. 
However, any expenditures made by the city for 
advertising or other campaign activities in opposition to the 
referendum would be considered independent expenditures by the 
City (see 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18420); therefore, we would 
consider the city to be an independent expenditure committee if 
expenditures of that sort totaled $500 or more in a calendar 
year. 
KED:plh

sections.question: Whether the city could become an independent expenditure committee which must file campaign statements if the City spends $500 or more for the preparation of ballot pamphlet arguments against a referendum for a redevelopment agency ordinance.
sections.conclusion: I informed Mr. Hutton that we would not consider the city to be an independent expenditure committee if it spends $500 or more for the preparation of ballot pamphlet arguments against the referendum. Elections Code Sections 4015 and 4057 specifically authorize the city council to submit arguments against a referendum for publication in the ballot pamphlet. Because this expenditure is specifically authorized by statute it is a legitimate expenditure for the performance of the official duties of the city council, and should not be considered an independent expenditure under Government Code section 82031. However, any expenditures made by the city for advertising or other campaign activities in opposition to the referendum would be considered independent expenditures by the City; therefore, we would consider the city to be an independent expenditure committee if expenditures of that sort totaled $500 or more in a calendar year.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a city file campaign statements as an independent expenditure committee if it spends $500 or more preparing arguments against a referendum for publication in the ballot pamphlet?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The city would not be considered an independent expenditure committee for ballot pamphlet arguments, as this is specifically authorized by statute as a legitimate official duty. However, any other campaign expenditures in opposition to the referendum would be considered independent expenditures, requiring campaign statement filing if they total $500 or more annually.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Telephone advice memorandum with clear question and conclusion sections. Document number M-85-219, dated October 25, 1985. Advice given by Kathryn E. Donovan to John Hutton, City Attorney of Kings County.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the city could become an independent expenditure committee which must file campaign statements if the City spends $500 or more for the preparation of ballot pamphlet arguments against a referendum for a redevelopment agency ordinance.

CONCLUSION: I informed Mr. Hutton that we would not consider the city to be an independent expenditure committee if it spends $500 or more for the preparation of ballot pamphlet arguments against the referendum. Elections Code Sections 4015 and 4057 specifically authorize the city council to submit arguments against a referendum for publication in the ballot pamphlet. Because this expenditure is specifically authorized by statute it is a legitimate expenditure for the performance of the official duties of the city council, and should not be considered an independent expenditure under Government Code section 82031. However, any expenditures made by the city for advertising or other campaign activities in opposition to the referendum would be considered independent expenditures by the City; therefore, we would consider the city to be an independent expenditure committee if expenditures of that sort totaled $500 or more in a calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to the City Attorney of Kings County regarding whether a city must file campaign statements as an independent expenditure committee when spending $500+ on ballot pamphlet arguments opposing a referendum. The FPPC concluded that ballot pamphlet arguments are statutorily authorized official duties and not independent expenditures, but other campaign activities opposing the referendum would trigger independent expenditure committee status.
